group_runs.title: Tuesday Night Intervals
group_runs.address: Ferry Field, Palmer Park, Argo Loop, B2B Trail, the Arb + more

group_runs.title: Saturday Morning Group Run
group_runs.distance: longer workouts on marked course · beginner + fast groups

events.title: A2SF Top of the Park — Swing Dance Lessons & Djangophonique
events.venue: Top of the Park (Ingalls Mall & Washington St)
events.description: Swing dance lessons at 5:30 PM followed by Djangophonique (Gypsy jazz) at 8:15 PM. Part of the Ann Arbor Summer Festival's free nightly programming running through June 28. All outdoor, all ages.

events.title: A2 Dance Revolution — Salsa Mondays
events.description: Weekly salsa classes at 6:30 PM (beginner Level 1 + Level 3 simultaneously, then Level 2 + Level 4 at 8 PM). Instructors Laura and Luis. 'No experience? No partner? No problem.' Runs every Monday.

events.title: A2 Dance Revolution — Bachata Wednesdays
events.description: Weekly bachata classes at 6:30 PM with social dancing after. Level 1 + Level 3 from 6:30–7:45 PM, Level 2 + Level 4 from 8–9:15 PM. Runs every Wednesday.

events.title: Ian Flanigan — The Ark
events.description: Country/Americana singer with 'worn leather' vocals — Voice finalist with a road-tested sound. One of The Ark's periodic free donation shows.

events.title: Esther Rose — The Ark
events.description: Country/pop artist on her fifth album, blending pop sensibilities with country narratives. Intimate venue, great sightlines.

events.title: Handmade Moments — The Ark
events.description: New Orleans duo playing American roots music with diverse instrumentation and tag-team beatboxing. Expect surprises.

events.title: The RFD Boys — The Ark
events.description: Legends of Michigan bluegrass, performing since 1969. A local institution at a local institution.

events.title: Matt Watroba — The Ark
events.description: Celebrating 40 years in radio and 50 years performing — a folk/roots Michigan legend. Sunday matinee-adjacent timing.

events.title: Holly Near with Jan Martinelli — The Ark
events.description: Peace-focused folk singer integrating spiritual and theatrical elements across a 50-year career. One of the most distinctive voices in American folk.

events.title: Pixie and the Partygrass Boys — The Ark
events.description: High-energy Utah bluegrass band known for their raucous, fun performances. Pre-4th-of-July energy.

events.title: A2SF Top of the Park — Open Stage & Film
events.venue: Top of the Park (Ingalls Mall & Washington St)
events.cost_notes: $3 for Open Stage open mic; outdoor film follows
events.description: The Ark's Open Stage (8-minute slots, open to all) at 8 PM. Followed by an outdoor film screening. Part of Top of the Park's nightly programming.

events.title: UMMA Gallery Visit — 'Zina Saro-Wiwa: Table Manners (Season 1)'
events.description: Digital displays exploring identity and cultural practice by British-Nigerian artist Zina Saro-Wiwa. Also on view: 'American Sampler: Activating the Archive' and 'We Write To You About Africa.' Free admission to all UMMA galleries.

events.title: Ann Arbor Art Fair
events.description: One of the nation's largest juried art fairs — nearly 1,000 artists across 30 city blocks. Three independently juried fairs running simultaneously: Street Art Fair, Summer Art Fair, State Street Art Fair. Thu Jul 16 10am–9pm, Fri Jul 17 10am–9pm, Sat Jul 18 10am–8pm. Last two days overlap with your stay.
